_comment_purpose: Hand-authored MANIFEST.json that lives at the root of a canonical corpus directory. Required by `synth seed` (scripts/synth/seed.py::seed_tenant) — the seeder reads it before walking <canonical_dir>/raw/<source>/*.json. Strip the `_comment_*` keys before use; the loader doesn't validate them but they're noise for tooling.
_comment_layout: Expected directory layout: <canonical_dir>/MANIFEST.json + <canonical_dir>/raw/<slack|notion|github|linear|sentry>/<event_id>.json. The example below assumes the corpus was recorded by running `synth run --record-llm --output-dir <canonical_dir>` against a profile whose customer_id matches `canonical_customer_id` here.
_comment_canonical_customer_id: REQUIRED. The customer_id used when the corpus was recorded. Must be a non-empty string. The seeder uses this to detect tenant-id rewrites needed when seeding a different customer (see seed.py around line 205).
_comment_version: OPTIONAL. Free-form string. Convention: bump when you re-record. Used by humans + tooling to know which canonical revision is active.
description: Canonical corpus recorded from prbe-knowledge with tiny_test preset, seed=1, regen.max_rounds=3. Includes plot archetypes (incident, launch, big_refactor) post-regen-loop. Replaces the templated-only canonical-v1 that Plan 4 V1 had to ship.
_comment_description: OPTIONAL. Free-form prose for the operator who finds this file in 6 months. No length limit; no consumer reads it.
_comment_envelope_counts: OPTIONAL. Per-source counts. Loaded but no consumer reads it today. Useful for sanity-checking that <canonical_dir>/raw/<source>/ contains what you expect.
_comment_recorded_at: OPTIONAL. ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. Convention only; no consumer reads it.
_comment_recording_profile: OPTIONAL. Path to the profile that produced this corpus. Convention only; no consumer reads it. Useful for reproducing the recording.
